reasoning: As of March 2025, Fabiano Caruana has dropped to #4 in the FIDE rankings with a rating of 2783. He is closely trailed by Arjun Erigaisi (2777) and Nodirbek Abdusattorov (2773), while trailing Gukesh (2787) and Nakamura (2802). The question asks if his ranking will be 'as high or higher' (i.e. rank 1, 2, 3, or 4) than his March 2025 ranking on several future dates.  Because FIDE updates its lists on the 1st of each month, the April 6 and April 29 resolution dates will both reference the April 1 list. Since there have been no major classical tournaments reported in March that definitively pushed Erigaisi or others past Caruana in the live ratings, he is highly likely to remain in the top 4 for the April updates. However, the proximity of Erigaisi and Abdusattorov (both within 10 points) introduces significant volatility over longer horizons. As young, highly active players, they have a substantial chance of surpassing Caruana within the next few months to a year. Conversely, Caruana could also regain points to overtake Gukesh or Nakamura, though the trend of the rising younger generation makes a hold on the top 4 less certain over time. Thus, the probability gradually falls towards and slightly below 50% for the distantly projected March 2026 date.
